View Code (7925 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead};
use std::f64::consts::PI;

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
struct Point {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
}

impl Point {
    fn new(x: f64, y: f64) -> Self {
        Point { x, y }
    }

    fn add(&self, other: &Point) -> Point {
        Point {
            x: self.x + other.x,
            y: self.y + other.y,
        }
    }

    fn rotate(&self, angle: f64) -> Point {
        let cos = angle.cos();
        let sin = angle.sin();
        Point {
            x: self.x * cos - self.y * sin,
            y: self.x * sin + self.y * cos,
        }
    }

    fn distance_to(&self, other: &Point) -> f64 {
        ((self.x - other.x).powi(2) + (self.y - other.y).powi(2)).sqrt()
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
struct Polygon {
    vertices: Vec<Point>,
}

impl Polygon {
    fn new(vertices: Vec<Point>) -> Self {
        Polygon { vertices }
    }

    fn translate(&self, offset: &Point) -> Polygon {
        let translated: Vec<Point> = self.vertices.iter().map(|v| v.add(offset)).collect();
        Polygon::new(translated)
    }

    fn rotate(&self, angle: f64) -> Polygon {
        let rotated: Vec<Point> = self.vertices.iter().map(|v| v.rotate(angle)).collect();
        Polygon::new(rotated)
    }

    fn transform(&self, angle: f64, offset: &Point) -> Polygon {
        self.rotate(angle).translate(offset)
    }

    fn bounding_box(&self) -> (f64, f64, f64, f64) {
        let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
        let mut max_x = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
        let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
        let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;

        for v in &self.vertices {
            min_x = min_x.min(v.x);
            max_x = max_x.max(v.x);
            min_y = min_y.min(v.y);
            max_y = max_y.max(v.y);
        }

        (min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y)
    }

    fn centroid(&self) -> Point {
        let sum_x: f64 = self.vertices.iter().map(|v| v.x).sum();
        let sum_y: f64 = self.vertices.iter().map(|v| v.y).sum();
        Point::new(sum_x / self.vertices.len() as f64, sum_y / self.vertices.len() as f64)
    }
}

fn point_in_polygon(point: &Point, polygon: &Polygon) -> bool {
    let mut inside = false;
    let verts = &polygon.vertices;
    let n = verts.len();

    for i in 0..n {
        let v1 = &verts[i];
        let v2 = &verts[(i + 1) % n];

        if (v1.y > point.y) != (v2.y > point.y) {
            let xinters = (v2.x - v1.x) * (point.y - v1.y) / (v2.y - v1.y) + v1.x;
            if point.x < xinters {
                inside = !inside;
            }
        }
    }

    inside
}

fn polygon_inside_polygon(inner: &Polygon, outer: &Polygon) -> bool {
    inner.vertices.iter().all(|v| point_in_polygon(v, outer))
}

fn segment_intersection(p1: &Point, p2: &Point, p3: &Point, p4: &Point) -> bool {
    let ccw = |a: &Point, b: &Point, c: &Point| -> bool {
        (c.y - a.y) * (b.x - a.x) > (b.y - a.y) * (c.x - a.x)
    };

    ccw(p1, p3, p4) != ccw(p2, p3, p4) && ccw(p1, p2, p3) != ccw(p1, p2, p4)
}

fn polygons_overlap(poly1: &Polygon, poly2: &Polygon) -> bool {
    let verts1 = &poly1.vertices;
    let verts2 = &poly2.vertices;

    for i in 0..verts1.len() {
        for j in 0..verts2.len() {
            if segment_intersection(
                &verts1[i],
                &verts1[(i + 1) % verts1.len()],
                &verts2[j],
                &verts2[(j + 1) % verts2.len()],
            ) {
                return true;
            }
        }
    }

    if let Some(first) = verts1.first() {
        if point_in_polygon(first, poly2) {
            return true;
        }
    }

    if let Some(first) = verts2.first() {
        if point_in_polygon(first, poly1) {
            return true;
        }
    }

    false
}

struct Placement {
    stock_idx: usize,
    offset: Point,
    rotation: f64,
}

fn find_placement(
    piece: &Polygon,
    stock: &Polygon,
    placed_pieces: &[Polygon],
    num_rotations: usize,
) -> Option<Placement> {
    let (min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y) = stock.bounding_box();
    let stock_width = max_x - min_x;
    let stock_height = max_y - min_y;

    let grid_size = (stock_width * stock_height / 100.0).sqrt().max(5.0).min(50.0) as usize;
    let step_x = stock_width / grid_size as f64;
    let step_y = stock_height / grid_size as f64;

    for rotation_idx in 0..num_rotations {
        let angle = (rotation_idx as f64) * PI * 2.0 / num_rotations as f64;
        let rotated = piece.rotate(angle);

        for i in 0..grid_size {
            for j in 0..grid_size {
                let test_x = min_x + step_x * (i as f64 + 0.5);
                let test_y = min_y + step_y * (j as f64 + 0.5);

                let candidate_centroid = rotated.centroid();
                let offset = Point::new(test_x - candidate_centroid.x, test_y - candidate_centroid.y);
                let transformed = rotated.translate(&offset);

                if !polygon_inside_polygon(&transformed, stock) {
                    continue;
                }

                let mut overlaps = false;
                for placed in placed_pieces {
                    if polygons_overlap(&transformed, placed) {
                        overlaps = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }

                if !overlaps {
                    return Some(Placement {
                        stock_idx: 0,
                        offset,
                        rotation: angle,
                    });
                }
            }
        }
    }

    None
}

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    let first_line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
    let parts: Vec<&str> = first_line.split_whitespace().collect();
    let sv: usize = parts[0].parse().unwrap();
    let np: usize = parts[1].parse().unwrap();

    let mut stock_vertices = Vec::new();
    for _ in 0..sv {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let coords: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace().map(|s| s.parse().unwrap()).collect();
        stock_vertices.push(Point::new(coords[0], coords[1]));
    }

    let stock_polygon = Polygon::new(stock_vertices);

    let mut pieces = Vec::new();
    for _ in 0..np {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let pv: usize = line.parse().unwrap();
        let mut piece_vertices = Vec::new();
        for _ in 0..pv {
            let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
            let coords: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace().map(|s| s.parse().unwrap()).collect();
            piece_vertices.push(Point::new(coords[0], coords[1]));
        }
        pieces.push(Polygon::new(piece_vertices));
    }

    let mut stocks = vec![Vec::new()];
    let mut placements = Vec::new();

    let num_rotations = if np > 1000 { 4 } else if np > 100 { 8 } else { 16 };

    for piece in &pieces {
        let mut placed = false;

        for (stock_idx, placed_pieces) in stocks.iter().enumerate() {
            if let Some(mut placement) = find_placement(piece, &stock_polygon, placed_pieces, num_rotations) {
                placement.stock_idx = stock_idx;
                placements.push(placement);
                stocks[stock_idx].push(piece.transform(placement.rotation, &placement.offset));
                placed = true;
                break;
            }
        }

        if !placed {
            stocks.push(Vec::new());
            let stock_idx = stocks.len() - 1;
            if let Some(mut placement) = find_placement(piece, &stock_polygon, &[], num_rotations) {
                placement.stock_idx = stock_idx;
                placements.push(placement);
                stocks[stock_idx].push(piece.transform(placement.rotation, &placement.offset));
            }
        }
    }

    println!("{}", stocks.len());
    for placement in placements {
        println!("{} {} {} {}", placement.stock_idx, placement.offset.x, placement.offset.y, placement.rotation);
    }
}

View Code (7925 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead};
use std::f64::consts::PI;

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
struct Point {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
}

impl Point {
    fn new(x: f64, y: f64) -> Self {
        Point { x, y }
    }

    fn add(&self, other: &Point) -> Point {
        Point {
            x: self.x + other.x,
            y: self.y + other.y,
        }
    }

    fn rotate(&self, angle: f64) -> Point {
        let cos = angle.cos();
        let sin = angle.sin();
        Point {
            x: self.x * cos - self.y * sin,
            y: self.x * sin + self.y * cos,
        }
    }

    fn distance_to(&self, other: &Point) -> f64 {
        ((self.x - other.x).powi(2) + (self.y - other.y).powi(2)).sqrt()
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
struct Polygon {
    vertices: Vec<Point>,
}

impl Polygon {
    fn new(vertices: Vec<Point>) -> Self {
        Polygon { vertices }
    }

    fn translate(&self, offset: &Point) -> Polygon {
        let translated: Vec<Point> = self.vertices.iter().map(|v| v.add(offset)).collect();
        Polygon::new(translated)
    }

    fn rotate(&self, angle: f64) -> Polygon {
        let rotated: Vec<Point> = self.vertices.iter().map(|v| v.rotate(angle)).collect();
        Polygon::new(rotated)
    }

    fn transform(&self, angle: f64, offset: &Point) -> Polygon {
        self.rotate(angle).translate(offset)
    }

    fn bounding_box(&self) -> (f64, f64, f64, f64) {
        let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
        let mut max_x = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
        let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
        let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;

        for v in &self.vertices {
            min_x = min_x.min(v.x);
            max_x = max_x.max(v.x);
            min_y = min_y.min(v.y);
            max_y = max_y.max(v.y);
        }

        (min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y)
    }

    fn centroid(&self) -> Point {
        let sum_x: f64 = self.vertices.iter().map(|v| v.x).sum();
        let sum_y: f64 = self.vertices.iter().map(|v| v.y).sum();
        Point::new(sum_x / self.vertices.len() as f64, sum_y / self.vertices.len() as f64)
    }
}

fn point_in_polygon(point: &Point, polygon: &Polygon) -> bool {
    let mut inside = false;
    let verts = &polygon.vertices;
    let n = verts.len();

    for i in 0..n {
        let v1 = &verts[i];
        let v2 = &verts[(i + 1) % n];

        if (v1.y > point.y) != (v2.y > point.y) {
            let xinters = (v2.x - v1.x) * (point.y - v1.y) / (v2.y - v1.y) + v1.x;
            if point.x < xinters {
                inside = !inside;
            }
        }
    }

    inside
}

fn polygon_inside_polygon(inner: &Polygon, outer: &Polygon) -> bool {
    inner.vertices.iter().all(|v| point_in_polygon(v, outer))
}

fn segment_intersection(p1: &Point, p2: &Point, p3: &Point, p4: &Point) -> bool {
    let ccw = |a: &Point, b: &Point, c: &Point| -> bool {
        (c.y - a.y) * (b.x - a.x) > (b.y - a.y) * (c.x - a.x)
    };

    ccw(p1, p3, p4) != ccw(p2, p3, p4) && ccw(p1, p2, p3) != ccw(p1, p2, p4)
}

fn polygons_overlap(poly1: &Polygon, poly2: &Polygon) -> bool {
    let verts1 = &poly1.vertices;
    let verts2 = &poly2.vertices;

    for i in 0..verts1.len() {
        for j in 0..verts2.len() {
            if segment_intersection(
                &verts1[i],
                &verts1[(i + 1) % verts1.len()],
                &verts2[j],
                &verts2[(j + 1) % verts2.len()],
            ) {
                return true;
            }
        }
    }

    if let Some(first) = verts1.first() {
        if point_in_polygon(first, poly2) {
            return true;
        }
    }

    if let Some(first) = verts2.first() {
        if point_in_polygon(first, poly1) {
            return true;
        }
    }

    false
}

struct Placement {
    stock_idx: usize,
    offset: Point,
    rotation: f64,
}

fn find_placement(
    piece: &Polygon,
    stock: &Polygon,
    placed_pieces: &[Polygon],
    num_rotations: usize,
) -> Option<Placement> {
    let (min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y) = stock.bounding_box();
    let stock_width = max_x - min_x;
    let stock_height = max_y - min_y;

    let grid_size = (stock_width * stock_height / 100.0).sqrt().max(5.0).min(50.0) as usize;
    let step_x = stock_width / grid_size as f64;
    let step_y = stock_height / grid_size as f64;

    for rotation_idx in 0..num_rotations {
        let angle = (rotation_idx as f64) * PI * 2.0 / num_rotations as f64;
        let rotated = piece.rotate(angle);

        for i in 0..grid_size {
            for j in 0..grid_size {
                let test_x = min_x + step_x * (i as f64 + 0.5);
                let test_y = min_y + step_y * (j as f64 + 0.5);

                let candidate_centroid = rotated.centroid();
                let offset = Point::new(test_x - candidate_centroid.x, test_y - candidate_centroid.y);
                let transformed = rotated.translate(&offset);

                if !polygon_inside_polygon(&transformed, stock) {
                    continue;
                }

                let mut overlaps = false;
                for placed in placed_pieces {
                    if polygons_overlap(&transformed, placed) {
                        overlaps = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }

                if !overlaps {
                    return Some(Placement {
                        stock_idx: 0,
                        offset,
                        rotation: angle,
                    });
                }
            }
        }
    }

    None
}

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    let first_line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
    let parts: Vec<&str> = first_line.split_whitespace().collect();
    let sv: usize = parts[0].parse().unwrap();
    let np: usize = parts[1].parse().unwrap();

    let mut stock_vertices = Vec::new();
    for _ in 0..sv {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let coords: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace().map(|s| s.parse().unwrap()).collect();
        stock_vertices.push(Point::new(coords[0], coords[1]));
    }

    let stock_polygon = Polygon::new(stock_vertices);

    let mut pieces = Vec::new();
    for _ in 0..np {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let pv: usize = line.parse().unwrap();
        let mut piece_vertices = Vec::new();
        for _ in 0..pv {
            let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
            let coords: Vec<f64> = line.split_whitespace().map(|s| s.parse().unwrap()).collect();
            piece_vertices.push(Point::new(coords[0], coords[1]));
        }
        pieces.push(Polygon::new(piece_vertices));
    }

    let mut stocks = vec![Vec::new()];
    let mut placements = Vec::new();

    let num_rotations = if np > 1000 { 4 } else if np > 100 { 8 } else { 16 };

    for piece in &pieces {
        let mut placed = false;

        for (stock_idx, placed_pieces) in stocks.iter().enumerate() {
            if let Some(mut placement) = find_placement(piece, &stock_polygon, placed_pieces, num_rotations) {
                placement.stock_idx = stock_idx;
                placements.push(placement);
                stocks[stock_idx].push(piece.transform(placement.rotation, &placement.offset));
                placed = true;
                break;
            }
        }

        if !placed {
            stocks.push(Vec::new());
            let stock_idx = stocks.len() - 1;
            if let Some(mut placement) = find_placement(piece, &stock_polygon, &[], num_rotations) {
                placement.stock_idx = stock_idx;
                placements.push(placement);
                stocks[stock_idx].push(piece.transform(placement.rotation, &placement.offset));
            }
        }
    }

    println!("{}", stocks.len());
    for placement in placements {
        println!("{} {} {} {}", placement.stock_idx, placement.offset.x, placement.offset.y, placement.rotation);
    }
}

Test 12: 3D Bin Packing - Polyhedra in Box (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Bin Packing problem in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small containers (5x5x5), simple polyhedra
- **Medium**: Medium containers (10x10x10-20x20x20)
- **Large**: Large containers (50x50x50-100x100x100)
- **Extreme**: Massive containers (200x200x200-500x500x500)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V F (vertex/face count of the polyhedron)
Next V lines: x y z (polyhedron vertices)
Next F lines: a b c (polyhedron face indices)
Last line: W H D (container width, height, depth)
Container origin at (0,0,0), extends to (W,H,D).

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: count (number of polyhedra packed)
Next count lines: tx ty tz qw qx qy qz
tx,ty,tz: translation
qw,qx,qy,qz: rotation quaternion (w is scalar part)

**Quaternion Rotation Reference:**
- Identity (no rotation): 1 0 0 0
- 90° around Z: 0.707 0 0 0.707
- To rotate a point: p' = q * p * q^(-1)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container size and polyhedron complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize packing count while ensuring valid placements

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.

Pack as many copies of an irregular 3D shape into a box as possible, without any overlaps or pieces poking outside. The shapes can be rotated freely, so finding the best orientations is part of the puzzle.

The AI must handle 3D collision detection and spatial reasoning. Subpasses use increasingly awkward shapes that resist neat stacking. The baseline uses a simple grid placement without rotation optimisation.

3D Bin Packing - 2x2x2 boxes in 10x10x10 container

View Code (9274 chars)
﻿using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class Vector3
{
    public double X, Y, Z;
    public Vector3(double x = 0, double y = 0, double z = 0) { X = x; Y = y; Z = z; }
    public Vector3 Add(Vector3 v) => new Vector3(X + v.X, Y + v.Y, Z + v.Z);
    public Vector3 Sub(Vector3 v) => new Vector3(X - v.X, Y - v.Y, Z - v.Z);
    public Vector3 Mul(double s) => new Vector3(X * s, Y * s, Z * s);
    public double Dot(Vector3 v) => X * v.X + Y * v.Y + Z * v.Z;
    public Vector3 Cross(Vector3 v) => new Vector3(Y * v.Z - Z * v.Y, Z * v.X - X * v.Z, X * v.Y - Y * v.X);
    public double Length() => Math.Sqrt(X * X + Y * Y + Z * Z);
    public Vector3 Normalize() { double len = Length(); return len > 1e-10 ? Mul(1 / len) : new Vector3(1, 0, 0); }
}

class Quaternion
{
    public double W, X, Y, Z;
    public Quaternion(double w = 1, double x = 0, double y = 0, double z = 0) { W = w; X = x; Y = y; Z = z; }

    public static Quaternion FromAxisAngle(Vector3 axis, double angle)
    {
        axis = axis.Normalize();
        double half = angle / 2;
        double s = Math.Sin(half);
        return new Quaternion(Math.Cos(half), axis.X * s, axis.Y * s, axis.Z * s);
    }

    public Vector3 RotatePoint(Vector3 p)
    {
        double x = W * p.X + Y * p.Z - Z * p.Y;
        double y = W * p.Y + Z * p.X - X * p.Z;
        double z = W * p.Z + X * p.Y - Y * p.X;
        double w = -X * p.X - Y * p.Y - Z * p.Z;

        return new Vector3(
            w * -X + x * W - y * Z + z * Y,
            w * -Y + y * W - z * X + x * Z,
            w * -Z + z * W - x * Y + y * X
        );
    }
}

class Polyhedron
{
    public Vector3[] Vertices;
    public int[][] Faces;
    public Vector3 MinBound, MaxBound;
    public Vector3 Center;

    public Polyhedron(Vector3[] vertices, int[][] faces)
    {
        Vertices = vertices;
        Faces = faces;
        ComputeBounds();
    }

    void ComputeBounds()
    {
        MinBound = new Vector3(double.MaxValue, double.MaxValue, double.MaxValue);
        MaxBound = new Vector3(double.MinValue, double.MinValue, double.MinValue);
        Vector3 sum = new Vector3();

        foreach (var v in Vertices)
        {
            MinBound = new Vector3(Math.Min(MinBound.X, v.X), Math.Min(MinBound.Y, v.Y), Math.Min(MinBound.Z, v.Z));
            MaxBound = new Vector3(Math.Max(MaxBound.X, v.X), Math.Max(MaxBound.Y, v.Y), Math.Max(MaxBound.Z, v.Z));
            sum = sum.Add(v);
        }
        Center = sum.Mul(1.0 / Vertices.Length);
    }

    public Polyhedron Transform(Vector3 translation, Quaternion rotation)
    {
        var newVerts = Vertices.Select(v => rotation.RotatePoint(v).Add(translation)).ToArray();
        return new Polyhedron(newVerts, Faces);
    }

    public bool FitsInContainer(double w, double h, double d)
    {
        var bounds = GetBounds();
        return bounds[0] >= -1e-6 && bounds[1] >= -1e-6 && bounds[2] >= -1e-6 &&
               bounds[3] <= w + 1e-6 && bounds[4] <= h + 1e-6 && bounds[5] <= d + 1e-6;
    }

    public double[] GetBounds()
    {
        double minX = double.MaxValue, minY = double.MaxValue, minZ = double.MaxValue;
        double maxX = double.MinValue, maxY = double.MinValue, maxZ = double.MinValue;

        foreach (var v in Vertices)
        {
            minX = Math.Min(minX, v.X); maxX = Math.Max(maxX, v.X);
            minY = Math.Min(minY, v.Y); maxY = Math.Max(maxY, v.Y);
            minZ = Math.Min(minZ, v.Z); maxZ = Math.Max(maxZ, v.Z);
        }
        return new[] { minX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ };
    }
}

class Placement
{
    public Vector3 Translation;
    public Quaternion Rotation;
    public Polyhedron Transformed;
}

class BinPacker
{
    Polyhedron basePolyhedron;
    double containerW, containerH, containerD;
    List<Placement> placements = new List<Placement>();

    public BinPacker(Polyhedron poly, double w, double h, double d)
    {
        basePolyhedron = poly;
        containerW = w;
        containerH = h;
        containerD = d;
    }

    public void Pack()
    {
        int scale = DetermineScale();

        switch (scale)
        {
            case 1: PackTrivial(); break;
            case 2: PackMedium(); break;
            case 3: PackLarge(); break;
            case 4: PackExtreme(); break;
        }
    }

    int DetermineScale()
    {
        double maxDim = Math.Max(containerW, Math.Max(containerH, containerD));
        if (maxDim <= 10) return 1;
        if (maxDim <= 30) return 2;
        if (maxDim <= 150) return 3;
        return 4;
    }

    void PackTrivial()
    {
        PackWithStrategy(new[] { new Quaternion(1, 0, 0, 0) }, 2);
    }

    void PackMedium()
    {
        var rotations = GenerateRotations(4);
        PackWithStrategy(rotations, 1.5);
    }

    void PackLarge()
    {
        var rotations = GenerateRotations(2);
        PackWithStrategy(rotations, 1);
    }

    void PackExtreme()
    {
        var rotations = new[] { new Quaternion(1, 0, 0, 0) };
        PackWithStrategy(rotations, 0.5);
    }

    Quaternion[] GenerateRotations(int count)
    {
        var rots = new List<Quaternion> { new Quaternion(1, 0, 0, 0) };

        for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
        {
            var axis = new Vector3(1, 1, 1).Normalize();
            rots.Add(Quaternion.FromAxisAngle(new Vector3(1, 0, 0), Math.PI / 2));
            rots.Add(Quaternion.FromAxisAngle(new Vector3(0, 1, 0), Math.PI / 2));
            rots.Add(Quaternion.FromAxisAngle(new Vector3(0, 0, 1), Math.PI / 2));
        }

        return rots.Distinct(new QuatComparer()).ToArray();
    }

    void PackWithStrategy(Quaternion[] rotations, double gridSpacing)
    {
        double step = Math.Max(gridSpacing, 0.5);
        bool improved = true;

        while (improved && placements.Count < 1000)
        {
            improved = false;
            for (double x = 0; x < containerW; x += step)
            {
                for (double y = 0; y < containerH; y += step)
                {
                    for (double z = 0; z < containerD; z += step)
                    {
                        var pos = new Vector3(x, y, z);
                        foreach (var rot in rotations)
                        {
                            if (TryPlace(pos, rot))
                            {
                                improved = true;
                                break;
                            }
                        }
                        if (improved) break;
                    }
                    if (improved) break;
                }
                if (improved) break;
            }
        }
    }

    bool TryPlace(Vector3 pos, Quaternion rot)
    {
        var transformed = basePolyhedron.Transform(pos, rot);

        if (!transformed.FitsInContainer(containerW, containerH, containerD))
            return false;

        foreach (var p in placements)
        {
            if (CollidesWith(transformed, p.Transformed))
                return false;
        }

        placements.Add(new Placement { Translation = pos, Rotation = rot, Transformed = transformed });
        return true;
    }

    bool CollidesWith(Polyhedron a, Polyhedron b)
    {
        var boundsA = a.GetBounds();
        var boundsB = b.GetBounds();

        double minDist = 1e-6;
        return !(boundsA[3] + minDist < boundsB[0] || boundsB[3] + minDist < boundsA[0] ||
                 boundsA[4] + minDist < boundsB[1] || boundsB[4] + minDist < boundsA[1] ||
                 boundsA[5] + minDist < boundsB[2] || boundsB[5] + minDist < boundsA[2]);
    }

    public void WriteSolution()
    {
        Console.WriteLine(placements.Count);
        foreach (var p in placements)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{p.Translation.X:F6} {p.Translation.Y:F6} {p.Translation.Z:F6} {p.Rotation.W:F6} {p.Rotation.X:F6} {p.Rotation.Y:F6} {p.Rotation.Z:F6}");
        }
    }
}

class QuatComparer : IEqualityComparer<Quaternion>
{
    public bool Equals(Quaternion a, Quaternion b) =>
        Math.Abs(a.W - b.W) < 1e-6 && Math.Abs(a.X - b.X) < 1e-6 &&
        Math.Abs(a.Y - b.Y) < 1e-6 && Math.Abs(a.Z - b.Z) < 1e-6;
    public int GetHashCode(Quaternion q) => q.W.GetHashCode();
}

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        string[] lines = System.IO.File.ReadAllLines("input.txt");
        var parts = lines[0].Split();
        int V = int.Parse(parts[0]), F = int.Parse(parts[1]);

        var vertices = new Vector3[V];
        for (int i = 0; i < V; i++)
        {
            var p = lines[i + 1].Split();
            vertices[i] = new Vector3(double.Parse(p[0]), double.Parse(p[1]), double.Parse(p[2]));
        }

        var faces = new int[F][];
        for (int i = 0; i < F; i++)
        {
            var p = lines[V + 1 + i].Split();
            faces[i] = new[] { int.Parse(p[0]), int.Parse(p[1]), int.Parse(p[2]) };
        }

        var containerParts = lines[V + F + 1].Split();
        double W = double.Parse(containerParts[0]);
        double H = double.Parse(containerParts[1]);
        double D = double.Parse(containerParts[2]);

        var poly = new Polyhedron(vertices, faces);
        var packer = new BinPacker(poly, W, H, D);
        packer.Pack();
        packer.WriteSolution();
    }
}

Test 13: Longest Common Substring (C++) Score: 0.00 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Longest Common Substring problem in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-3 short strings (10-50 chars each)
- **Medium**: 3-5 medium strings (100-500 chars each)
- **Large**: 5-10 long strings (1000-5000 chars each)
- **Extreme**: 100 very long strings (10MB+ each), massive search space

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of strings)
Next N lines: one string per line

**Output format (stdout):**
One line: the longest common substring
If no common substring exists, output an empty line.

**Example:**
Input:
3
abcdef
xbcdey
zbcdew

Output:
bcde

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number and length of strings
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for very long strings
3. **Correctness**: Must find the actual longest common substring

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given several strings, find the longest chunk of text that appears identically in every one of them. For example, if the strings are “abcdef” and “xbcdey”, the answer is “bcde”.

Naïve approaches check every possible substring and become far too slow on large inputs. Subpasses increase the string lengths into the millions, so the AI must use efficient data structures or hashing. The baseline uses a simple library call that times out on the bigger cases.

 0.0000
[3 short strings (7 chars)] Invalid: Result must be string, got dict

Longest Common Substring - 3 short strings (7 chars)

View Code (2288 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>
#include <chrono>
#include <climits>

using namespace std;

auto g_startTime = chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();

bool isTimeout(int seconds = 280) {
    auto now = chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();
    return chrono::duration_cast<chrono::seconds>(now - g_startTime).count() > seconds;
}

bool hasCommonSubstringOfLength(const vector<string>& strings, int len, string& found) {
    if (strings.empty() || isTimeout()) return false;

    // Use shortest string as source for efficiency
    int srcIdx = 0;
    for (int i = 1; i < (int)strings.size(); i++) {
        if (strings[i].length() < strings[srcIdx].length()) {
            srcIdx = i;
        }
    }

    const string& src = strings[srcIdx];
    if ((int)src.length() < len) return false;

    for (int i = 0; i <= (int)src.length() - len; i++) {
        string sub = src.substr(i, len);
        bool inAll = true;

        for (int j = 0; j < (int)strings.size(); j++) {
            if (j == srcIdx) continue;
            if (strings[j].find(sub) == string::npos) {
                inAll = false;
                break;
            }
        }

        if (inAll) {
            found = sub;
            return true;
        }
    }

    return false;
}

string solve(const vector<string>& strings) {
    if (strings.empty()) return "";

    // Find maximum possible length
    int maxLen = INT_MAX;
    for (const auto& s : strings) {
        maxLen = min(maxLen, (int)s.length());
    }

    if (maxLen == INT_MAX) maxLen = 0;

    int left = 1, right = maxLen;
    string result = "";

    while (left <= right) {
        int mid = (left + right) / 2;
        string found;

        if (hasCommonSubstringOfLength(strings, mid, found)) {
            result = found;
            left = mid + 1;
        } else {
            right = mid - 1;
        }
    }

    return result;
}

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    g_startTime = chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();

    int n;
    cin >> n;
    cin.ignore();

    vector<string> strings(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        getline(cin, strings[i]);
    }

    string result = solve(strings);
    cout << result << endl;

    return 0;
}

Test 14: Minesweeper Solver (C#) Score: 0.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a Minesweeper solver in C# that must handle ANY board complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

- **Trivial**: Small boards (5x5-8x8), simple patterns, few mines
- **Extreme**: Massive boards (10000x10000), very complex constraint satisfaction problems

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the board row strings (each W characters)

**Cell meanings:**
- ' ' (space): Unknown/unrevealed cell - could be mine or safe
- '0'-'8': Revealed safe cell showing count of adjacent mines (8 neighbors)
- '*': Flagged mine (known mine location)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of safe cells found)
Next N lines: x y (column row, 0-indexed)
Only output cells that are currently unknown (' ') and provably safe.
Do NOT guess - only return cells you can prove are safe.

**Example:**
For a board where cell (0,0) shows '1' and has one adjacent mine at (1,0):
Neighbors (0,1) and (0,2) are guaranteed safe.

**Constraints:**
- Return as many provably safe cells as possible
- Never return a cell that could be a mine
- Must handle varying board sizes and mine densities efficiently

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on board complexity.

Given a partially-revealed Minesweeper board, deduce which hidden cells are safe to click and which contain mines. Each visible number tells you exactly how many of its neighbours are mined — the AI must combine these clues to make guaranteed-safe moves without guessing.

Larger boards and denser mine layouts make the logic harder. The problem is NP-complete in general, but clever constraint reasoning can solve most practical boards. The baseline uses only the simplest single-cell deduction rules.

 0.0000
[8x8 board, 10 mines] Compilation failed:
Determining projects to restore...
Restored D:\CodingBenchmark\compile_cache\claude-haiku-4-5-MaxReasoning-Tools\csharp\project_fc882467cdbf305a\Program.csproj (in 148 ms).
CSC

Minesweeper Solver - 8x8 board, 10 mines

View Code (4020 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;

class MineSweeper
{
    private int height, width;
    private Dictionary<(int, int), char> board;
    private HashSet<(int, int)> knownMines;
    private HashSet<(int, int)> unknownCells;

    static void Main()
    {
        new MineSweeper().Solve();
    }

    void Solve()
    {
        ReadInput();
        PropagateSafeCells();
        OutputResult();
    }

    void ReadInput()
    {
        var line = Console.ReadLine().Split();
        height = int.Parse(line[0]);
        width = int.Parse(line[1]);

        board = new Dictionary<(int, int), char>();
        knownMines = new HashSet<(int, int)>();
        unknownCells = new HashSet<(int, int)>();

        for (int row = 0; row < height; row++)
        {
            string input = Console.ReadLine();
            for (int col = 0; col < width; col++)
            {
                char cell = input[col];
                if (cell == ' ')
                {
                    unknownCells.Add((col, row));
                }
                else
                {
                    board[(col, row)] = cell;
                    if (cell == '*')
                    {
                        knownMines.Add((col, row));
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    void PropagateSafeCells()
    {
        bool changed = true;
        while (changed)
        {
            changed = false;
            var cellsToProcess = new List<(int, int)>(board.Keys);

            foreach (var (col, row) in cellsToProcess)
            {
                char cellChar = board[(col, row)];
                if (cellChar < '0' || cellChar > '8') continue;

                int mineCount = cellChar - '0';
                var neighbors = GetNeighbors(col, row);

                int knownCount = 0;
                var unknownNeighbors = new List<(int, int)>();

                foreach (var neighbor in neighbors)
                {
                    if (knownMines.Contains(neighbor))
                    {
                        knownCount++;
                    }
                    else if (unknownCells.Contains(neighbor))
                    {
                        unknownNeighbors.Add(neighbor);
                    }
                }

                // All mines accounted for - remaining unknowns are safe
                if (knownCount == mineCount && unknownNeighbors.Count > 0)
                {
                    foreach (var cell in unknownNeighbors)
                    {
                        unknownCells.Remove(cell);
                    }
                    changed = true;
                }

                // Mines equal unknowns - all unknowns must be mines
                else if (knownCount + unknownNeighbors.Count == mineCount && unknownNeighbors.Count > 0)
                {
                    foreach (var cell in unknownNeighbors)
                    {
                        knownMines.Add(cell);
                        unknownCells.Remove(cell);
                    }
                    changed = true;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    List<(int, int)> GetNeighbors(int col, int row)
    {
        var result = new List<(int, int)>(8);
        for (int dr = -1; dr <= 1; dr++)
        {
            for (int dc = -1; dc <= 1; dc++)
            {
                if (dr == 0 && dc == 0) continue;
                int nc = col + dc;
                int nr = row + dr;
                if (nr >= 0 && nr < height && nc >= 0 && nc < width)
                {
                    result.Add((nc, nr));
                }
            }
        }
        return result;
    }

    void OutputResult()
    {
        var cells = new List<(int, int)>(unknownCells);
        cells.Sort((a, b) => a.Item1 == b.Item1 ? a.Item2.CompareTo(b.Item2) : a.Item1.CompareTo(b.Item1));

        Console.WriteLine(cells.Count);
        foreach (var (col, row) in cells)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{col} {row}");
        }
    }
}

Test 15: Tetrahedron Shadow Covering (Rust) Score: 5.70 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Shadow Covering problem with tetrahedrons in Rust.

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with polygons ranging from simple shapes to very complex geometries.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: NV (number of polygon vertices)
Next NV lines: x y (polygon vertices on z=0 plane)
Last line: sx sy sz (sun direction vector, points from sun toward ground)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: count (number of tetrahedrons)
Next count lines: px py pz qw qx qy qz scale
px,py,pz: center position
qw,qx,qy,qz: rotation quaternion (w is scalar)
scale: uniform scale factor

**Standard Tetrahedron Reference:**
Vertices (before transform):
[1.0, 0.0, -0.707]
[-1.0, 0.0, -0.707]
[0.0, 1.0, 0.707]
[0.0, -1.0, 0.707]

**Constraints:**
- Combined shadows must completely cover target polygon
- Tetrahedrons must not intersect each other in 3D
- Maximum scale factor per tetrahedron: 10.0
- Minimize number of tetrahedrons

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Be aware that default warnings are enabled and will cause a compilation failure,
so ensure that you write warning-free code.

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.

Place 3D tetrahedra (triangular pyramids) above a flat surface so that their shadows, cast by a directional light, completely cover a given 2D target shape. Use as few tetrahedra as possible while covering every part of the target.

The AI must reason about 3D-to-2D projection, rotation, and coverage geometry. Subpasses use more complex target shapes and trickier light angles. The baseline uses a simple grid of tetrahedra above the target.

 1.0000
[3x3 square, sun straight down] 100% coverage with 1 tetrahedrons, Time: 0.1s

Shadow Covering - 3x3 square, sun straight down

View Code (6369 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct Vec3 {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct Quaternion {
    w: f64,
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct Point2D {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
}

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut lines = stdin.lock().lines();

    let nv: usize = lines
        .next()
        .unwrap()
        .unwrap()
        .trim()
        .parse()
        .unwrap();

    let mut polygon = Vec::new();
    for _ in 0..nv {
        let line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
        let parts: Vec<&str> = line.trim().split_whitespace().collect();
        let x: f64 = parts[0].parse().unwrap();
        let y: f64 = parts[1].parse().unwrap();
        polygon.push(Point2D { x, y });
    }

    let sun_line = lines.next().unwrap().unwrap();
    let sun_parts: Vec<&str> = sun_line.trim().split_whitespace().collect();
    let mut sun_dir = Vec3 {
        x: sun_parts[0].parse().unwrap(),
        y: sun_parts[1].parse().unwrap(),
        z: sun_parts[2].parse().unwrap(),
    };

    let sun_len = (sun_dir.x * sun_dir.x + sun_dir.y * sun_dir.y + sun_dir.z * sun_dir.z).sqrt();
    if sun_len > 1e-10 {
        sun_dir.x /= sun_len;
        sun_dir.y /= sun_len;
        sun_dir.z /= sun_len;
    }

    let tetrahedrons = solve_covering(&polygon, sun_dir);

    println!("{}", tetrahedrons.len());
    for (pos, quat, scale) in tetrahedrons {
        println!(
            "{} {} {} {} {} {} {} {}",
            pos.x, pos.y, pos.z, quat.w, quat.x, quat.y, quat.z, scale
        );
    }
}

fn ref_tetrahedron_vertices() -> Vec<Vec3> {
    vec![
        Vec3 {
            x: 1.0,
            y: 0.0,
            z: -0.707,
        },
        Vec3 {
            x: -1.0,
            y: 0.0,
            z: -0.707,
        },
        Vec3 {
            x: 0.0,
            y: 1.0,
            z: 0.707,
        },
        Vec3 {
            x: 0.0,
            y: -1.0,
            z: 0.707,
        },
    ]
}

fn solve_covering(
    polygon: &[Point2D],
    sun_dir: Vec3,
) -> Vec<(Vec3, Quaternion, f64)> {
    let mut result = Vec::new();

    if polygon.is_empty() {
        return result;
    }

    let bbox = compute_bbox(polygon);
    let width = bbox.max_x - bbox.min_x;
    let height = bbox.max_y - bbox.min_y;

    let target_scale = 3.0;
    let cell_size = target_scale * 1.5;
    let grid_cols = ((width / cell_size).ceil() as usize).max(1);
    let grid_rows = ((height / cell_size).ceil() as usize).max(1);

    let col_step = if grid_cols > 0 {
        width / (grid_cols as f64)
    } else {
        cell_size
    };
    let row_step = if grid_rows > 0 {
        height / (grid_rows as f64)
    } else {
        cell_size
    };

    let ref_verts = ref_tetrahedron_vertices();

    for row in 0..grid_rows {
        for col in 0..grid_cols {
            let cx = bbox.min_x + (col as f64 + 0.5) * col_step;
            let cy = bbox.min_y + (row as f64 + 0.5) * row_step;
            let test_point = Point2D { x: cx, y: cy };

            if point_in_polygon(&test_point, polygon) {
                let scale = (target_scale).min(10.0);
                let shadow = compute_shadow(&ref_verts, cx, cy, 0.5, scale, sun_dir);

                if shadow_area(&shadow) > 1e-6 {
                    let quat = Quaternion {
                        w: 1.0,
                        x: 0.0,
                        y: 0.0,
                        z: 0.0,
                    };
                    result.push((Vec3 { x: cx, y: cy, z: 0.5 }, quat, scale));
                }
            }
        }
    }

    if result.is_empty() && !polygon.is_empty() {
        let centroid = polygon_centroid(polygon);
        let scale = 3.0_f64.min(10.0);
        let quat = Quaternion {
            w: 1.0,
            x: 0.0,
            y: 0.0,
            z: 0.0,
        };
        result.push((
            Vec3 {
                x: centroid.x,
                y: centroid.y,
                z: 0.5,
            },
            quat,
            scale,
        ));
    }

    result
}

fn compute_bbox(polygon: &[Point2D]) -> BBox {
    let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut max_x = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
    let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;

    for p in polygon {
        min_x = min_x.min(p.x);
        max_x = max_x.max(p.x);
        min_y = min_y.min(p.y);
        max_y = max_y.max(p.y);
    }

    BBox {
        min_x,
        max_x,
        min_y,
        max_y,
    }
}

struct BBox {
    min_x: f64,
    max_x: f64,
    min_y: f64,
    max_y: f64,
}

fn point_in_polygon(p: &Point2D, polygon: &[Point2D]) -> bool {
    let mut inside = false;
    let n = polygon.len();
    let mut j = n - 1;

    for i in 0..n {
        let pi = polygon[i];
        let pj = polygon[j];

        if ((pi.y > p.y) != (pj.y > p.y))
            && (p.x < (pj.x - pi.x) * (p.y - pi.y) / (pj.y - pi.y) + pi.x)
        {
            inside = !inside;
        }
        j = i;
    }

    inside
}

fn polygon_centroid(polygon: &[Point2D]) -> Point2D {
    let mut cx = 0.0;
    let mut cy = 0.0;
    for p in polygon {
        cx += p.x;
        cy += p.y;
    }
    let n = polygon.len() as f64;
    Point2D {
        x: cx / n,
        y: cy / n,
    }
}

fn compute_shadow(
    ref_verts: &[Vec3],
    px: f64,
    py: f64,
    pz: f64,
    scale: f64,
    sun_dir: Vec3,
) -> Vec<Point2D> {
    let mut shadow = Vec::new();

    for v in ref_verts {
        let scaled_x = v.x * scale;
        let scaled_y = v.y * scale;
        let scaled_z = v.z * scale;

        let world_x = px + scaled_x;
        let world_y = py + scaled_y;
        let world_z = pz + scaled_z;

        if sun_dir.z.abs() > 1e-10 {
            let t = -world_z / sun_dir.z;
            let proj_x = world_x + t * sun_dir.x;
            let proj_y = world_y + t * sun_dir.y;
            shadow.push(Point2D {
                x: proj_x,
                y: proj_y,
            });
        }
    }

    shadow
}

fn shadow_area(shadow: &[Point2D]) -> f64 {
    if shadow.len() < 3 {
        return 0.0;
    }

    let mut area = 0.0;
    for i in 0..shadow.len() {
        let j = (i + 1) % shadow.len();
        area += shadow[i].x * shadow[j].y;
        area -= shadow[j].x * shadow[i].y;
    }
    (area / 2.0).abs()
}

Test 16: Job-Shop Scheduling (C++) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Job-Shop Scheduling problem in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to extremely large scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-3 jobs, 2-3 machines, 5-10 tasks total
- **Extreme**: 1000+ jobs, 50+ machines, 50k+ tasks total

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: J M (number of jobs, number of machines)
For each job:
Line: T (number of tasks in this job)
Next T lines: machine duration

**Output format (stdout):**
For each job (J lines total):
T pairs per line: start_time machine start_time machine ... (all tasks for that job)

**Example:**
Input:
2 2
2
0 3
1 2
2
1 2
0 4

Output:
0 0 3 1
0 1 5 0

(Job 0: task 0 starts at t=0 on M0, task 1 at t=3 on M1)
(Job 1: task 0 starts at t=0 on M1, task 1 at t=5 on M0)

**Key Constraints:**
1. Each task in a job must run on its specified machine
2. Tasks within a job must execute in order (task i before task i+1)
3. A machine can only run one task at a time
4. Tasks cannot be interrupted once started

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Schedule a set of jobs across several machines to finish everything as quickly as possible. Each job is a sequence of tasks that must run in order, and each task needs a specific machine — but a machine can only do one thing at a time.

This classic factory-scheduling problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number of jobs and machines. The baseline greedily starts whichever task can begin earliest.

 0.0000
[3 jobs, 2 machines] Makespan: 16, Baseline: 12, Time: 0.2s - valid but inefficient (1.33x baseline)

Job-Shop Scheduling - 3 jobs, 2 machines

View Code (1632 chars)
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

using namespace std;

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int J, M;
    cin >> J >> M;

    vector<vector<pair<int, int>>> jobs(J);

    // Read input: for each job, store (machine, duration) pairs for each task
    for (int j = 0; j < J; j++) {
        int T;
        cin >> T;
        for (int t = 0; t < T; t++) {
            int machine, duration;
            cin >> machine >> duration;
            jobs[j].push_back({machine, duration});
        }
    }

    // Track when each machine becomes free
    vector<long long> machine_free_time(M, 0);

    // Schedule each job and output results
    for (int j = 0; j < J; j++) {
        long long job_completion_time = 0;

        for (int t = 0; t < jobs[j].size(); t++) {
            int machine = jobs[j][t].first;
            int duration = jobs[j][t].second;

            // Task must start after:
            // 1. Previous task in this job completes
            // 2. Machine becomes available
            long long start_time = max(job_completion_time, machine_free_time[machine]);
            long long end_time = start_time + duration;

            // Output: space-separated pairs of (start_time machine) for each task
            if (t > 0) cout << " ";
            cout << start_time << " " << machine;

            // Update when this machine next becomes free
            machine_free_time[machine] = end_time;

            // Update when this job's tasks are done
            job_completion_time = end_time;
        }
        cout << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 17: 3D AABB Bin Packing (C#) Score: 0.00 / 8
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Bin Packing problem with axis-aligned boxes in C#.

Your program must work on all scales, from 10,000 boxes in 1,000,000 cubic units
down to 4 boxes in a 10x10x10 container.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N W H D (number of boxes, container width, height, depth)
Next N lines: w h d (box dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: packed_count
Next packed_count lines: box_index x y z (0-indexed box index and corner position)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container size and number of boxes
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for very large containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of boxes packed while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Boxes must be entirely within container bounds
- No two boxes may overlap
- Boxes are axis-aligned (no arbitrary rotation, but you may reorder w/h/d)
- Maximize number of boxes packed

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack a collection of rectangular boxes into a shipping container, fitting as much total volume in as possible. Boxes may be rotated to any axis-aligned orientation, but cannot overlap or stick out of the container.

This 3D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of box sizes. The baseline uses a greedy first-fit approach.

 0.0000
[4 boxes in 10x10x10] Compilation failed:
Determining projects to restore...
Restored D:\CodingBenchmark\compile_cache\claude-haiku-4-5-MaxReasoning-Tools\csharp\project_583743a83d0f5320\Program.csproj (in 211 ms).
CSC

3D AABB Packing - 4 boxes in 10x10x10

View Code (4282 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class Program
{
    struct Box
    {
        public int Index;
        public int W, H, D;
    }

    struct Placement
    {
        public int Index;
        public int X, Y, Z;
        public int W, H, D;
    }

    static void Main()
    {
        var line = Console.ReadLine().Split();
        int n = int.Parse(line[0]);
        int W = int.Parse(line[1]);
        int H = int.Parse(line[2]);
        int D = int.Parse(line[3]);

        var boxes = new Box[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            var dims = Console.ReadLine().Split();
            boxes[i] = new Box
            {
                Index = i,
                W = int.Parse(dims[0]),
                H = int.Parse(dims[1]),
                D = int.Parse(dims[2])
            };
        }

        // Sort by volume descending (largest first)
        var sortedBoxes = boxes.OrderByDescending(b => (long)b.W * b.H * b.D).ToArray();

        var placements = new List<Placement>();
        var candidates = new HashSet<(int, int, int)> { (0, 0, 0) };

        foreach (var box in sortedBoxes)
        {
            var orientations = GetOrientations(box);
            bool placed = false;

            // Try candidates sorted by position (leftmost, bottom-most, front-most)
            var sortedCandidates = candidates.OrderBy(c => c.Item1).ThenBy(c => c.Item2).ThenBy(c => c.Item3).ToList();

            foreach (var oriented in orientations)
            {
                foreach (var (x, y, z) in sortedCandidates)
                {
                    // Check if box fits within container bounds
                    if (x + oriented.W > W || y + oriented.H > H || z + oriented.D > D)
                        continue;

                    // Check for overlaps with already placed boxes
                    bool overlaps = false;
                    foreach (var p in placements)
                    {
                        if (BoxesOverlap(x, y, z, oriented.W, oriented.H, oriented.D,
                                       p.X, p.Y, p.Z, p.W, p.H, p.D))
                        {
                            overlaps = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }

                    // Place the box if no overlap
                    if (!overlaps)
                    {
                        var placement = new Placement { Index = oriented.Index, X = x, Y = y, Z = z, W = oriented.W, H = oriented.H, D = oriented.D };
                        placements.Add(placement);

                        // Add new candidate positions from the corners of this placed box
                        candidates.Add((x + oriented.W, y, z));
                        candidates.Add((x, y + oriented.H, z));
                        candidates.Add((x, y, z + oriented.D));

                        placed = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }

                if (placed) break;
            }
        }

        // Output results
        Console.WriteLine(placements.Count);
        var result = placements.OrderBy(p => p.Index).ToList();
        foreach (var p in result)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{p.Index} {p.X} {p.Y} {p.Z}");
        }
    }

    // Generate all unique orientations (permutations of dimensions)
    static List<Box> GetOrientations(Box box)
    {
        var dims = new[] { box.W, box.H, box.D };
        var orientations = new HashSet<(int, int, int)>();

        // Try all permutations of dimensions
        foreach (var w in dims)
            foreach (var h in dims)
                foreach (var d in dims)
                    orientations.Add((w, h, d));

        return orientations.Select(o => new Box
        {
            Index = box.Index,
            W = o.Item1,
            H = o.Item2,
            D = o.Item3
        }).ToList();
    }

    // Check if two axis-aligned boxes overlap
    static bool BoxesOverlap(int x1, int y1, int z1, int w1, int h1, int d1,
                            int x2, int y2, int z2, int w2, int h2, int d2)
    {
        return x1 < x2 + w2 && x1 + w1 > x2 &&
               y1 < y2 + h2 && y1 + h1 > y2 &&
               z1 < z2 + d2 && z1 + d1 > z2;
    }
}

Test 18: Minimum Cut Problem (C++) Score: 0.00 / 9
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a C++ program to solve the Minimum Edge Cut problem.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler information**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to multi-gb.

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ program will be tested with graphs ranging from small toy examples to massive real-world networks.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Problem:**
Given an undirected connected graph, find the minimum number of edges to remove
such that the graph becomes disconnected (split into two non-empty components).

**Input format (stdin):**
- Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
- Lines 2 to M+1: u v (undirected edge between nodes u and v, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
- Line 1: K (number of edges in the minimum cut)
- Lines 2 to K+1: u v (each edge in the cut, in any order)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with this compiler, and run on this environment.
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle graphs even if they don't fit in memory.
4. Complete as fast as possible.
5. Output must be a valid cut that disconnects the graph
6. Minimize the number of cut edges
7. Must handle varying graph sizes efficiently

**Example:**
Input:
```
4 5
0 1
1 2
2 3
3 0
0 2
```

Output (one possible answer):
```
2
0 1
3 0
```

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different algorithms based on graph scale.
 Prompt Changes:

Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the smallest set of edges to remove from a graph so that it splits into two disconnected pieces. Think of it like finding the weakest point in a network — the fewest cables you’d need to cut to divide it in two.

Subpasses increase the graph size from small networks to very large ones. The AI must implement an efficient algorithm; naïve approaches are far too slow on the bigger inputs.

 0.0000
[6 nodes, 10 edges, min cut ~2] Cut size: 7, Known min: 2, Time: 0.61s - Suboptimal (3.5x optimal)

Subpass 0: 6 nodes, 10 edges, min cut ~2

Score: 0.00

Details: [6 nodes, 10 edges, min cut ~2] Cut size: 7, Known min: 2, Time: 0.61s - Suboptimal (3.5x optimal)

C++ Code 
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <algorithm>
#include <cstring>

using namespace std;

const int INF = 1e9;

struct Edge {
    int to, cap, rev;
};

class Dinic {
public:
    vector<vector<Edge>> graph;
    vector<int> level, iter;
    int n;

    Dinic(int n) : n(n), graph(n), level(n), iter(n) {}

    void addEdge(int from, int to, int cap) {
        graph[from].push_back({to, cap, (int)graph[to].size()});
        graph[to].push_back({from, 0, (int)graph[from].size() - 1});
    }

    bool bfs(int s, int t) {
        fill(level.begin(), level.end(), -1);
        queue<int> q;
        level[s] = 0;
        q.push(s);

        while (!q.empty()) {
            int v = q.front();
            q.pop();
            for (auto& e : graph[v]) {
                if (level[e.to] < 0 && e.cap > 0) {
                    level[e.to] = level[v] + 1;
                    q.push(e.to);
                }
            }
        }
        return level[t] >= 0;
    }

    int dfs(int v, int t, int f) {
        if (v == t) return f;
        for (int& i = iter[v]; i < (int)graph[v].size(); i++) {
            Edge& e = graph[v][i];
            if (level[v] < level[e.to] && e.cap > 0) {
                int d = dfs(e.to, t, min(f, e.cap));
                if (d > 0) {
                    e.cap -= d;
                    graph[e.to][e.rev].cap += d;
                    return d;
                }
            }
        }
        return 0;
    }

    int maxflow(int s, int t) {
        int flow = 0;
        while (bfs(s, t)) {
            fill(iter.begin(), iter.end(), 0);
            int f;
            while ((f = dfs(s, t, INF)) > 0) {
                flow += f;
            }
        }
        return flow;
    }
};

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m;
    cin >> n >> m;

    vector<pair<int, int>> edges(m);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
        cin >> edges[i].first >> edges[i].second;
    }

    if (m == 0) {
        cout << "0\n";
        return 0;
    }

    int minCut = INT_MAX;
    int bestTarget = 1;

    int maxTries = min(n - 1, 50);
    for (int target = 1; target <= maxTries; target++) {
        Dinic flow(n);
        for (auto [u, v] : edges) {
            flow.addEdge(u, v, 1);
            flow.addEdge(v, u, 1);
        }
        int cut = flow.maxflow(0, target);
        if (cut < minCut) {
            minCut = cut;
            bestTarget = target;
        }
    }

    Dinic flow(n);
    for (auto [u, v] : edges) {
        flow.addEdge(u, v, 1);
        flow.addEdge(v, u, 1);
    }
    flow.maxflow(0, bestTarget);

    vector<pair<int, int>> cutEdges;
    for (auto [u, v] : edges) {
        bool inCut = false;

        for (auto& e : flow.graph[u]) {
            if (e.to == v && e.cap == 0) {
                inCut = true;
                break;
            }
        }

        if (!inCut) {
            for (auto& e : flow.graph[v]) {
                if (e.to == u && e.cap == 0) {
                    inCut = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        if (inCut) {
            cutEdges.push_back({u, v});
        }
    }

    cout << cutEdges.size() << "\n";
    for (auto [u, v] : cutEdges) {
        cout << u << " " << v << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

C++ Code 
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
#include <algorithm>
#include <cstring>

using namespace std;

const int INF = 1e9;

struct Edge {
    int to, cap, rev;
};

class Dinic {
public:
    vector<vector<Edge>> graph;
    vector<int> level, iter;
    int n;

    Dinic(int n) : n(n), graph(n), level(n), iter(n) {}

    void addEdge(int from, int to, int cap) {
        graph[from].push_back({to, cap, (int)graph[to].size()});
        graph[to].push_back({from, 0, (int)graph[from].size() - 1});
    }

    bool bfs(int s, int t) {
        fill(level.begin(), level.end(), -1);
        queue<int> q;
        level[s] = 0;
        q.push(s);

        while (!q.empty()) {
            int v = q.front();
            q.pop();
            for (auto& e : graph[v]) {
                if (level[e.to] < 0 && e.cap > 0) {
                    level[e.to] = level[v] + 1;
                    q.push(e.to);
                }
            }
        }
        return level[t] >= 0;
    }

    int dfs(int v, int t, int f) {
        if (v == t) return f;
        for (int& i = iter[v]; i < (int)graph[v].size(); i++) {
            Edge& e = graph[v][i];
            if (level[v] < level[e.to] && e.cap > 0) {
                int d = dfs(e.to, t, min(f, e.cap));
                if (d > 0) {
                    e.cap -= d;
                    graph[e.to][e.rev].cap += d;
                    return d;
                }
            }
        }
        return 0;
    }

    int maxflow(int s, int t) {
        int flow = 0;
        while (bfs(s, t)) {
            fill(iter.begin(), iter.end(), 0);
            int f;
            while ((f = dfs(s, t, INF)) > 0) {
                flow += f;
            }
        }
        return flow;
    }
};

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int n, m;
    cin >> n >> m;

    vector<pair<int, int>> edges(m);
    for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
        cin >> edges[i].first >> edges[i].second;
    }

    if (m == 0) {
        cout << "0\n";
        return 0;
    }

    int minCut = INT_MAX;
    int bestTarget = 1;

    int maxTries = min(n - 1, 50);
    for (int target = 1; target <= maxTries; target++) {
        Dinic flow(n);
        for (auto [u, v] : edges) {
            flow.addEdge(u, v, 1);
            flow.addEdge(v, u, 1);
        }
        int cut = flow.maxflow(0, target);
        if (cut < minCut) {
            minCut = cut;
            bestTarget = target;
        }
    }

    Dinic flow(n);
    for (auto [u, v] : edges) {
        flow.addEdge(u, v, 1);
        flow.addEdge(v, u, 1);
    }
    flow.maxflow(0, bestTarget);

    vector<pair<int, int>> cutEdges;
    for (auto [u, v] : edges) {
        bool inCut = false;

        for (auto& e : flow.graph[u]) {
            if (e.to == v && e.cap == 0) {
                inCut = true;
                break;
            }
        }

        if (!inCut) {
            for (auto& e : flow.graph[v]) {
                if (e.to == u && e.cap == 0) {
                    inCut = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        if (inCut) {
            cutEdges.push_back({u, v});
        }
    }

    cout << cutEdges.size() << "\n";
    for (auto [u, v] : cutEdges) {
        cout << u << " " << v << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 19: 3D Voxel Mining (C#) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a C# program to solve a mining problem in a simplified 3D voxel space.

**Constraints:**
1. Can only dig dirt that is exposed from above or a side.
2. Dug dirt must be dumped somewhere - at a location not blocking future digging
3. Maximum uphill slope - 1 Z voxel per X/Y voxel.
4. Dumped dirt becomes a new obstacle (plan carefully!)
5. Goal is to get valuable ore to a processing plant located at x=0, y=0
6. Ground is stable and solid, and you can dig under any voxel without fear of it collapsing
so long as it's connected to the earth. If you disconnect a voxel(s), it will collapse.

The voxel grid size has 10x more precision in Z than X/Y. So 10x10x100 is a cube.

**Input header format (stdin - space delimited. Ends with newline):**
```
XVoxelCount YVoxelCount ZVoxelCount XBlockSize YBlockSize ZBlockSize
```
Counts are human readable integers. BlockSize are human readable decimal numbers.

After the newline, voxels then follow in bytes, like this python psuedocode:
```
for x in range(XVoxelCount):
for y in range(YVoxelCount):
for z in range(ZVoxelCount):
d = readByte(stdin)
massInTonnes = d % 16
valuePerTonne = d // 16
addVoxel(d)
```

Each peice of dirt needs to be moved to its new location - you need to trace the
path in X and Y (4-connected / manhatten steps) and ensure that the path doesn't climb uphill
or downhill more than 1 Z voxel for every X/Y voxel.

**Output format (stdout / newline seperated):**
```
M (number of operations)
N digX digY digZ stepX1 stepY1 stepX2 stepY2 (N steps) destX destY (for each operation)
```

If dumping valuable ore at the processing plant, the voxel ceases to exist. If a voxel is
dumped elsewhere, it is dumped on top of the ground, raising the ground by 1 voxel. It can
be dug again and moved later if required.

Every unit of mass requires $1 of fuel to move one voxel in X or Y when flat, or $5 of fuel
to go up a 10% slope. Downhill is free.

Getting valuable ore to your processing plant pays $1000 per unit of mass per unit of value.
The most valuable ore (#15) pays $15,000 per ton, so the most a single dump truck load can gross
is $225,000.

(Dumping junk ore at the processing plant causes damage to the plant, costing $1000 per ton.)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. World size could be anything from 10x10x10 to 5000x5000x5000, your code should handle both extremes.
4. Your code should complete in under 5 minutes
5. Maximize profit.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

Plan an open-pit mine in a 3D block model. Each block has a value (ore worth money, waste costs money to move) and you can only dig a block once the blocks above it have been removed. The goal is to maximise profit by choosing which blocks to extract and where to dump the waste.

The AI must balance revenue against haulage costs, respect slope constraints for road access, and avoid dumping waste where it blocks future digging. Subpasses increase the pit size and complexity.

3D Voxel Mining - 10×10 tiny pit (10 KB)

View C# Code (6962 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class Mining
{
    struct Voxel
    {
        public int mass;
        public int value;
    }

    struct Operation
    {
        public int x, y, z;
        public List<(int, int)> path;
        public int destX, destY;
    }

    static Voxel[,,] voxels;
    static bool[,,] removed;
    static int xCount, yCount, zCount;
    static double xBlockSize, yBlockSize, zBlockSize;

    static void Main()
    {
        string header = Console.ReadLine();
        var parts = header.Split(' ');
        xCount = int.Parse(parts[0]);
        yCount = int.Parse(parts[1]);
        zCount = int.Parse(parts[2]);
        xBlockSize = double.Parse(parts[3]);
        yBlockSize = double.Parse(parts[4]);
        zBlockSize = double.Parse(parts[5]);

        voxels = new Voxel[xCount, yCount, zCount];
        removed = new bool[xCount, yCount, zCount];

        // Read voxel data
        for (int x = 0; x < xCount; x++)
        {
            for (int y = 0; y < yCount; y++)
            {
                for (int z = 0; z < zCount; z++)
                {
                    int b = Console.In.Read();
                    voxels[x, y, z].mass = b % 16;
                    voxels[x, y, z].value = b / 16;
                }
            }
        }

        var operations = new List<Operation>();

        // Greedy strategy: prioritize high-value ore that's accessible
        // Build priority list of voxels to extract
        var candidates = new List<(int x, int y, int z, double profit)>();

        for (int x = 0; x < xCount; x++)
        {
            for (int y = 0; y < yCount; y++)
            {
                for (int z = 0; z < zCount; z++)
                {
                    if (!removed[x, y, z] && voxels[x, y, z].mass > 0)
                    {
                        // Check if exposed
                        if (IsExposed(x, y, z))
                        {
                            int mass = voxels[x, y, z].mass;
                            int value = voxels[x, y, z].value;

                            // Calculate profit: revenue - fuel cost
                            double revenue = mass * value * 1000.0;
                            double fuelCost = EstimateFuelCost(x, y, z);
                            double profit = revenue - fuelCost;

                            if (profit > 0 || value > 0) // Extract valuable ore or profitable ore
                            {
                                candidates.Add((x, y, z, profit));
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        candidates.Sort((a, b) => b.profit.CompareTo(a.profit));

        // Process candidates
        foreach (var (x, y, z, profit) in candidates.Take(Math.Min(candidates.Count, 10000)))
        {
            if (!removed[x, y, z] && IsExposed(x, y, z))
            {
                var path = FindPath(x, y, z, 0, 0);
                if (path != null && path.Count > 0)
                {
                    var op = new Operation
                    {
                        x = x,
                        y = y,
                        z = z,
                        path = path,
                        destX = 0,
                        destY = 0
                    };
                    operations.Add(op);
                    removed[x, y, z] = true;
                }
            }
        }

        // Output
        Console.WriteLine(operations.Count);
        foreach (var op in operations)
        {
            Console.Write($"{op.path.Count} {op.x} {op.y} {op.z}");
            foreach (var step in op.path)
            {
                Console.Write($" {step.Item1} {step.Item2}");
            }
            Console.WriteLine($" {op.destX} {op.destY}");
        }
    }

    static bool IsExposed(int x, int y, int z)
    {
        // Check if voxel is exposed from above or sides
        if (z == zCount - 1) return true; // Top is exposed

        // Check if exposed from above
        if (z + 1 < zCount && (removed[x, y, z + 1] || voxels[x, y, z + 1].mass == 0))
            return true;

        // Check sides
        int[] dx = { 1, -1, 0, 0 };
        int[] dy = { 0, 0, 1, -1 };

        for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++)
        {
            int nx = x + dx[i];
            int ny = y + dy[i];

            if (nx >= 0 && nx < xCount && ny >= 0 && ny < yCount)
            {
                if (removed[nx, ny, z] || voxels[nx, ny, z].mass == 0)
                    return true;
            }
            else if (nx < 0 || nx >= xCount || ny < 0 || ny >= yCount)
            {
                return true; // Edge is exposed
            }
        }

        return false;
    }

    static double EstimateFuelCost(int x, int y, int z)
    {
        int mass = voxels[x, y, z].mass;
        int dist = x + y; // Manhattan distance to (0,0)

        // Simple estimate: mostly flat movement + some slope
        double cost = dist * mass * 1.0;

        return cost;
    }

    static List<(int, int)> FindPath(int fromX, int fromY, int fromZ, int toX, int toY)
    {
        var path = new List<(int, int)>();

        // BFS with slope constraint
        var queue = new Queue<(int x, int y, int z, List<(int, int)> p)>();
        var visited = new HashSet<(int, int, int)>();

        queue.Enqueue((fromX, fromY, fromZ, path));
        visited.Add((fromX, fromY, fromZ));

        while (queue.Count > 0)
        {
            var (x, y, z, curPath) = queue.Dequeue();

            if (x == toX && y == toY)
            {
                return curPath;
            }

            // Try 4 directions
            int[] dx = { 1, -1, 0, 0 };
            int[] dy = { 0, 0, 1, -1 };

            for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++)
            {
                int nx = x + dx[i];
                int ny = y + dy[i];

                if (nx < 0 || nx >= xCount || ny < 0 || ny >= yCount)
                    continue;

                // Find ground level at new position
                int nz = z;
                for (int checkZ = z; checkZ >= 0; checkZ--)
                {
                    if (!removed[nx, ny, checkZ] && voxels[nx, ny, checkZ].mass > 0)
                    {
                        nz = checkZ;
                        break;
                    }
                    if (checkZ == 0) nz = -1;
                }

                if (nz < 0) nz = 0;

                // Check slope constraint
                if (Math.Abs(nz - z) > 1)
                    continue;

                if (!visited.Contains((nx, ny, nz)))
                {
                    visited.Add((nx, ny, nz));
                    var newPath = new List<(int, int)>(curPath);
                    newPath.Add((nx, ny));
                    queue.Enqueue((nx, ny, nz, newPath));
                }
            }
        }

        return path.Count > 0 ? path : null;
    }
}
