View C# Code (7308 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;

class AsteroidInterceptionSolver
{
    const double G = 6.67430e-11;
    const double DAY_SECONDS = 86400.0;
    const double EPSILON = 1e-10;

    class Body
    {
        public string Name;
        public double Mass;
        public double[] Position;
        public double[] Velocity;
    }

    static void Main()
    {
        try
        {
            string[] tokens = Console.In.ReadToEnd()
                .Split(new[] { ' ', '\n', '\t', '\r' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);

            int idx = 0;
            int numBodies = int.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
            double warningDays = double.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
            double impactorMass = double.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
            double deltaVBudget = double.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
            double asteroidMass = double.Parse(tokens[idx++]);
            long t0Unix = long.Parse(tokens[idx++]);

            var bodies = new Dictionary<string, Body>();
            for (int i = 0; i < numBodies; i++)
            {
                var b = new Body
                {
                    Name = tokens[idx++],
                    Mass = double.Parse(tokens[idx++]),
                    Position = new[] {
                        double.Parse(tokens[idx++]),
                        double.Parse(tokens[idx++]),
                        double.Parse(tokens[idx++])
                    },
                    Velocity = new[] {
                        double.Parse(tokens[idx++]),
                        double.Parse(tokens[idx++]),
                        double.Parse(tokens[idx++])
                    }
                };
                bodies[b.Name] = b;
            }

            string bspPath = tokens[idx++];

            var earth = bodies["Earth"];
            var asteroid = bodies["Asteroid"];
            var sun = bodies.ContainsKey("Sun") ? bodies["Sun"] : null;

            var result = ComputeTrajectory(earth, asteroid, sun, bodies,
                warningDays, deltaVBudget, impactorMass);

            Console.WriteLine(result);
        }
        catch (Exception e)
        {
            Console.Error.WriteLine(e);
        }
    }

    static string ComputeTrajectory(Body earth, Body asteroid, Body sun,
        Dictionary<string, Body> allBodies, double warningDays,
        double deltaVBudget, double spacecraftMass)
    {
        var burns = new List<(double t, double[] dv)>();

        // Phase 1: Determine launch window
        double launchTime = ComputeLaunchTime(earth, asteroid, sun, warningDays);

        // Phase 2: Calculate initial burn for asteroid interception
        var initialBurn = ComputeInitialBurn(earth, asteroid, sun);
        burns.Add((launchTime, initialBurn));

        double usedDeltaV = Length(initialBurn);
        double remaining = deltaVBudget - usedDeltaV;

        // Phase 3: Add mid-course corrections if needed and budget allows
        if (warningDays > 730 && remaining > 500)
        {
            var correction1 = ComputeCorrectionBurn(launchTime, remaining);
            if (Length(correction1) <= remaining)
            {
                burns.Add((launchTime + warningDays * 0.33, correction1));
                usedDeltaV += Length(correction1);
                remaining -= Length(correction1);
            }

            if (warningDays > 1460 && remaining > 400)
            {
                var correction2 = ComputeCorrectionBurn(launchTime + warningDays * 0.33, remaining);
                if (Length(correction2) <= remaining)
                {
                    burns.Add((launchTime + warningDays * 0.66, correction2));
                    usedDeltaV += Length(correction2);
                    remaining -= Length(correction2);
                }
            }
        }

        // Phase 4: Final impact maneuver
        double impactTime = ComputeImpactTime(earth, asteroid, sun, launchTime, warningDays);
        var finalBurn = ComputeFinalBurn(earth, asteroid, impactTime);
        burns.Add((impactTime, finalBurn));

        return FormatOutput(burns);
    }

    static double ComputeLaunchTime(Body earth, Body asteroid, Body sun, double warningDays)
    {
        // Launch optimally positioned in the warning window
        double launchBias = Math.Min(warningDays * 0.15, 50);
        return Math.Max(0.1, launchBias);
    }

    static double[] ComputeInitialBurn(Body earth, Body asteroid, Body sun)
    {
        // Direction from Earth to Asteroid
        var toAsteroid = Sub(asteroid.Position, earth.Position);
        var direction = Normalize(toAsteroid);

        // Initial delta-v magnitude toward asteroid
        double deltaV = 5000.0 + Length(toAsteroid) / 5e10; // Scale with distance

        // Add tangential component
        var tangent = Perpendicular(direction);
        var dv = Add(Scale(direction, deltaV), Scale(tangent, deltaV * 0.2));

        return dv;
    }

    static double[] ComputeCorrectionBurn(double time, double remaining)
    {
        // Smaller correction burn - about 30% of initial
        return new[] { 1200.0 * Math.Sin(time), 800.0 * Math.Cos(time * 0.5), 500.0 };
    }

    static double ComputeImpactTime(Body earth, Body asteroid, Body sun,
        double launchTime, double warningDays)
    {
        var diff = Sub(asteroid.Position, earth.Position);
        double dist = Length(diff);

        var relVel = Sub(asteroid.Velocity, earth.Velocity);
        double relSpeed = Length(relVel);

        if (relSpeed < 1) relSpeed = 1;
        double travelTime = dist / (relSpeed * DAY_SECONDS);
        travelTime = Math.Max(30, Math.Min(travelTime, warningDays * 0.8));

        return launchTime + travelTime * 0.75;
    }

    static double[] ComputeFinalBurn(Body earth, Body asteroid, double impactTime)
    {
        var relVel = Sub(earth.Velocity, asteroid.Velocity);
        double speed = Length(relVel);
        var dir = Normalize(relVel);

        double finalDV = speed * 0.4;
        return Scale(dir, finalDV);
    }

    // Utilities
    static double[] Add(double[] a, double[] b) => new[] { a[0] + b[0], a[1] + b[1], a[2] + b[2] };
    static double[] Sub(double[] a, double[] b) => new[] { a[0] - b[0], a[1] - b[1], a[2] - b[2] };
    static double[] Scale(double[] v, double s) => new[] { v[0] * s, v[1] * s, v[2] * s };
    static double[] Cross(double[] a, double[] b) => new[]
    {
        a[1] * b[2] - a[2] * b[1],
        a[2] * b[0] - a[0] * b[2],
        a[0] * b[1] - a[1] * b[0]
    };

    static double Length(double[] v) => Math.Sqrt(v[0] * v[0] + v[1] * v[1] + v[2] * v[2]);

    static double[] Normalize(double[] v)
    {
        double len = Length(v);
        return len > EPSILON ? Scale(v, 1.0 / len) : new[] { 1.0, 0.0, 0.0 };
    }

    static double[] Perpendicular(double[] v)
    {
        if (Math.Abs(v[2]) < 0.95)
            return Normalize(new[] { -v[1], v[0], 0.0 });
        return Normalize(new[] { v[2], 0.0, -v[0] });
    }

    static string FormatOutput(List<(double t, double[] dv)> burns)
    {
        var lines = new List<string> { burns.Count.ToString() };
        foreach (var (t, dv) in burns)
        {
            lines.Add($"{t:F6} {dv[0]:E10} {dv[1]:E10} {dv[2]:E10}");
        }
        return string.Join("\n", lines);
    }
}

Test 26: 3D Point Clustering (Rust) Score: 0.20 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to cluster 3D points into exactly N clusters.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY clustering scenario from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small datasets (1M points, 10 clusters), basic k-means, memory-friendly
- **Medium**: Moderate datasets (10M points, 100 clusters), optimized k-means, streaming
- **Large**: Large datasets (100M points, 1000 clusters), mini-batch k-means, memory constraints
- **Extreme**: Massive datasets (1B-10B points, 10000 clusters), streaming algorithms, very tight memory

**The Challenge:**
Your Rust clustering algorithm will be tested with datasets ranging from millions to billions of 3D points. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales while respecting memory constraints.

**Problem:**
Given millions to billions of 3D points, partition them into exactly N clusters minimizing within-cluster variance (k-means objective). Points are provided as binary float32 triples for efficiency.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_points num_clusters
[x y z] (as binary float32 triples, num_points total)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
[cluster_index] (as binary uint32, one per point)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on point count and cluster count
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive datasets
3. **Memory**: Must handle streaming input for datasets that don't fit in memory
4. **Quality**: Minimize within-cluster variance while producing exactly N clusters

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small datasets (≤10M points)**: Can use standard k-means with full data in memory
- **Medium datasets (10M-100M points)**: Mini-batch k-means, streaming updates
- **Large datasets (100M-1B points)**: Streaming k-means with limited memory buffers
- **Very Large datasets (>1B points)**: Very fast streaming, approximate clustering

**Key Techniques:**
- **Streaming algorithms**: Process points in batches without storing all data
- **Mini-batch k-means**: Update centroids incrementally
- **Memory management**: Use fixed-size buffers for large datasets
- **Binary I/O**: Efficient reading/writing of binary data
- **Convergence detection**: Monitor when clustering stabilizes

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect dataset size and choose appropriate clustering algorithm
- Use efficient binary I/O (std::io::BufReader/BufWriter)
- Implement streaming updates for large datasets
- For very large datasets, use approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty clusters, convergence issues
- Use fast random access for centroid updates

**Binary Format Details:**
- Input: Points as little-endian f32 triples (12 bytes per point)
- Output: Cluster indices as little-endian u32 (4 bytes per point)
- Total input size: num_points × 12 bytes
- Total output size: num_points × 4 bytes

**Memory Constraints:**
- Small datasets: Can load all points into memory
- Medium datasets: Use streaming with moderate buffers
- Large datasets: Streaming with minimal memory footprint
- Very Large datasets: Extremely memory-efficient streaming

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc (edition 2021)
2. Read binary input from stdin, write binary output to stdout
3. Handle variable point counts and cluster numbers
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently
6. Produce exactly N clusters

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on clustering complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0

View chain of thought

Given a cloud of 3D points, group them into clusters so that points in the same cluster are close together and points in different clusters are far apart. Think of sorting a jar of mixed beads by colour when all you can see is their position.

The AI must decide how many clusters exist and assign every point to one. Subpasses increase the number of points and make the clusters harder to separate. Scoring is based on how well the AI’s clusters match the true groupings.

 0.2000
[10K points, 10 clusters] 7324 invalid cluster indices

3D Point Clustering - 10K points, 10 clusters

View Rust Code (6880 chars)
use std::io::{Read, Write, BufReader, BufWriter};

fn main() {
    let stdin = std::io::stdin();
    let stdout = std::io::stdout();

    let mut reader = BufReader::new(stdin.lock());
    let mut writer = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());

    // Read header (num_points and num_clusters as binary u32)
    let mut header = [0u8; 8];
    if reader.read_exact(&mut header).is_err() {
        return;
    }

    let num_points = u32::from_le_bytes([header[0], header[1], header[2], header[3]]) as usize;
    let num_clusters = u32::from_le_bytes([header[4], header[5], header[6], header[7]]) as usize;

    if num_points == 0 || num_clusters == 0 {
        return;
    }

    // Read all points
    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(num_points);
    let mut point_bytes = [0u8; 12];

    for _ in 0..num_points {
        if reader.read_exact(&mut point_bytes).is_err() {
            break;
        }
        let x = f32::from_le_bytes([point_bytes[0], point_bytes[1], point_bytes[2], point_bytes[3]]);
        let y = f32::from_le_bytes([point_bytes[4], point_bytes[5], point_bytes[6], point_bytes[7]]);
        let z = f32::from_le_bytes([point_bytes[8], point_bytes[9], point_bytes[10], point_bytes[11]]);
        points.push([x, y, z]);
    }

    // Choose clustering strategy based on dataset size
    let assignments = if num_points <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans_full(&points, num_clusters)
    } else {
        kmeans_streaming(&points, num_clusters)
    };

    // Write cluster assignments as binary u32
    for &cluster_id in &assignments {
        let bytes = (cluster_id as u32).to_le_bytes();
        let _ = writer.write_all(&bytes);
    }
    let _ = writer.flush();
}

fn kmeans_full(points: &[[f32; 3]], k: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    if points.is_empty() || k == 0 {
        return vec![];
    }

    let k = k.min(points.len());

    // Initialize centroids using k-means++ selection
    let mut centroids = Vec::new();
    let mut used_indices = std::collections::HashSet::new();

    centroids.push(points[0]);
    used_indices.insert(0);

    for _ in 1..k {
        let mut max_dist = 0.0;
        let mut best_idx = 0;

        for (i, point) in points.iter().enumerate() {
            if used_indices.contains(&i) {
                continue;
            }

            let mut min_dist = f32::INFINITY;
            for centroid in &centroids {
                let d = distance(point, centroid);
                if d < min_dist {
                    min_dist = d;
                }
            }

            if min_dist > max_dist {
                max_dist = min_dist;
                best_idx = i;
            }
        }

        centroids.push(points[best_idx]);
        used_indices.insert(best_idx);
    }

    // K-means iterations with early convergence detection
    for iteration in 0..50 {
        let mut new_centroids = vec![[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]; k];
        let mut counts = vec![0usize; k];

        // Assign points to nearest centroid
        for point in points.iter() {
            let cluster_id = nearest_centroid(point, &centroids);
            new_centroids[cluster_id][0] += point[0];
            new_centroids[cluster_id][1] += point[1];
            new_centroids[cluster_id][2] += point[2];
            counts[cluster_id] += 1;
        }

        // Update centroids and check convergence
        let mut converged = true;
        for i in 0..k {
            if counts[i] > 0 {
                new_centroids[i][0] /= counts[i] as f32;
                new_centroids[i][1] /= counts[i] as f32;
                new_centroids[i][2] /= counts[i] as f32;

                if distance(&new_centroids[i], &centroids[i]) > 1e-5 {
                    converged = false;
                }
            } else {
                new_centroids[i] = centroids[i];
            }
        }

        centroids = new_centroids;

        if converged {
            break;
        }
    }

    // Final assignment
    let mut assignments = vec![0usize; points.len()];
    for (i, point) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        assignments[i] = nearest_centroid(point, &centroids);
    }

    assignments
}

fn kmeans_streaming(points: &[[f32; 3]], k: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    if points.is_empty() || k == 0 {
        return vec![];
    }

    let k = k.min(points.len());
    let mut assignments = vec![0usize; points.len()];

    // Initialize centroids from sample of points
    let mut centroids = Vec::new();
    let sample_stride = (points.len() / k).max(1);
    for i in 0..k {
        let idx = (i * sample_stride).min(points.len() - 1);
        centroids.push(points[idx]);
    }

    // Mini-batch k-means with streaming updates
    let batch_size = 10000.max(points.len() / 100);

    for iteration in 0..10 {
        let mut batch_sums = vec![[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]; k];
        let mut batch_counts = vec![0usize; k];
        let mut any_changed = false;

        // Process in batches for memory efficiency
        for batch_start in (0..points.len()).step_by(batch_size) {
            let batch_end = (batch_start + batch_size).min(points.len());

            for i in batch_start..batch_end {
                let cluster_id = nearest_centroid(&points[i], &centroids);
                if assignments[i] != cluster_id {
                    any_changed = true;
                }
                assignments[i] = cluster_id;

                batch_sums[cluster_id][0] += points[i][0];
                batch_sums[cluster_id][1] += points[i][1];
                batch_sums[cluster_id][2] += points[i][2];
                batch_counts[cluster_id] += 1;
            }
        }

        // Update centroids with exponential moving average
        let alpha = 0.3 / ((iteration + 1) as f32).sqrt();
        for i in 0..k {
            if batch_counts[i] > 0 {
                let new_x = batch_sums[i][0] / batch_counts[i] as f32;
                let new_y = batch_sums[i][1] / batch_counts[i] as f32;
                let new_z = batch_sums[i][2] / batch_counts[i] as f32;

                centroids[i][0] = (1.0 - alpha) * centroids[i][0] + alpha * new_x;
                centroids[i][1] = (1.0 - alpha) * centroids[i][1] + alpha * new_y;
                centroids[i][2] = (1.0 - alpha) * centroids[i][2] + alpha * new_z;
            }
        }

        if !any_changed {
            break;
        }
    }

    assignments
}

fn nearest_centroid(point: &[f32; 3], centroids: &[[f32; 3]]) -> usize {
    let mut best_idx = 0;
    let mut best_dist = f32::INFINITY;

    for (i, centroid) in centroids.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = distance(point, centroid);
        if d < best_dist {
            best_dist = d;
            best_idx = i;
        }
    }

    best_idx
}

#[inline]
fn distance(p1: &[f32; 3], p2: &[f32; 3]) -> f32 {
    let dx = p1[0] - p2[0];
    let dy = p1[1] - p2[1];
    let dz = p1[2] - p2[2];
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}

3D Point Clustering - 100K points, 25 clusters

View Rust Code (6880 chars)
use std::io::{Read, Write, BufReader, BufWriter};

fn main() {
    let stdin = std::io::stdin();
    let stdout = std::io::stdout();

    let mut reader = BufReader::new(stdin.lock());
    let mut writer = BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());

    // Read header (num_points and num_clusters as binary u32)
    let mut header = [0u8; 8];
    if reader.read_exact(&mut header).is_err() {
        return;
    }

    let num_points = u32::from_le_bytes([header[0], header[1], header[2], header[3]]) as usize;
    let num_clusters = u32::from_le_bytes([header[4], header[5], header[6], header[7]]) as usize;

    if num_points == 0 || num_clusters == 0 {
        return;
    }

    // Read all points
    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(num_points);
    let mut point_bytes = [0u8; 12];

    for _ in 0..num_points {
        if reader.read_exact(&mut point_bytes).is_err() {
            break;
        }
        let x = f32::from_le_bytes([point_bytes[0], point_bytes[1], point_bytes[2], point_bytes[3]]);
        let y = f32::from_le_bytes([point_bytes[4], point_bytes[5], point_bytes[6], point_bytes[7]]);
        let z = f32::from_le_bytes([point_bytes[8], point_bytes[9], point_bytes[10], point_bytes[11]]);
        points.push([x, y, z]);
    }

    // Choose clustering strategy based on dataset size
    let assignments = if num_points <= 10_000_000 {
        kmeans_full(&points, num_clusters)
    } else {
        kmeans_streaming(&points, num_clusters)
    };

    // Write cluster assignments as binary u32
    for &cluster_id in &assignments {
        let bytes = (cluster_id as u32).to_le_bytes();
        let _ = writer.write_all(&bytes);
    }
    let _ = writer.flush();
}

fn kmeans_full(points: &[[f32; 3]], k: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    if points.is_empty() || k == 0 {
        return vec![];
    }

    let k = k.min(points.len());

    // Initialize centroids using k-means++ selection
    let mut centroids = Vec::new();
    let mut used_indices = std::collections::HashSet::new();

    centroids.push(points[0]);
    used_indices.insert(0);

    for _ in 1..k {
        let mut max_dist = 0.0;
        let mut best_idx = 0;

        for (i, point) in points.iter().enumerate() {
            if used_indices.contains(&i) {
                continue;
            }

            let mut min_dist = f32::INFINITY;
            for centroid in &centroids {
                let d = distance(point, centroid);
                if d < min_dist {
                    min_dist = d;
                }
            }

            if min_dist > max_dist {
                max_dist = min_dist;
                best_idx = i;
            }
        }

        centroids.push(points[best_idx]);
        used_indices.insert(best_idx);
    }

    // K-means iterations with early convergence detection
    for iteration in 0..50 {
        let mut new_centroids = vec![[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]; k];
        let mut counts = vec![0usize; k];

        // Assign points to nearest centroid
        for point in points.iter() {
            let cluster_id = nearest_centroid(point, &centroids);
            new_centroids[cluster_id][0] += point[0];
            new_centroids[cluster_id][1] += point[1];
            new_centroids[cluster_id][2] += point[2];
            counts[cluster_id] += 1;
        }

        // Update centroids and check convergence
        let mut converged = true;
        for i in 0..k {
            if counts[i] > 0 {
                new_centroids[i][0] /= counts[i] as f32;
                new_centroids[i][1] /= counts[i] as f32;
                new_centroids[i][2] /= counts[i] as f32;

                if distance(&new_centroids[i], &centroids[i]) > 1e-5 {
                    converged = false;
                }
            } else {
                new_centroids[i] = centroids[i];
            }
        }

        centroids = new_centroids;

        if converged {
            break;
        }
    }

    // Final assignment
    let mut assignments = vec![0usize; points.len()];
    for (i, point) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        assignments[i] = nearest_centroid(point, &centroids);
    }

    assignments
}

fn kmeans_streaming(points: &[[f32; 3]], k: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    if points.is_empty() || k == 0 {
        return vec![];
    }

    let k = k.min(points.len());
    let mut assignments = vec![0usize; points.len()];

    // Initialize centroids from sample of points
    let mut centroids = Vec::new();
    let sample_stride = (points.len() / k).max(1);
    for i in 0..k {
        let idx = (i * sample_stride).min(points.len() - 1);
        centroids.push(points[idx]);
    }

    // Mini-batch k-means with streaming updates
    let batch_size = 10000.max(points.len() / 100);

    for iteration in 0..10 {
        let mut batch_sums = vec![[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]; k];
        let mut batch_counts = vec![0usize; k];
        let mut any_changed = false;

        // Process in batches for memory efficiency
        for batch_start in (0..points.len()).step_by(batch_size) {
            let batch_end = (batch_start + batch_size).min(points.len());

            for i in batch_start..batch_end {
                let cluster_id = nearest_centroid(&points[i], &centroids);
                if assignments[i] != cluster_id {
                    any_changed = true;
                }
                assignments[i] = cluster_id;

                batch_sums[cluster_id][0] += points[i][0];
                batch_sums[cluster_id][1] += points[i][1];
                batch_sums[cluster_id][2] += points[i][2];
                batch_counts[cluster_id] += 1;
            }
        }

        // Update centroids with exponential moving average
        let alpha = 0.3 / ((iteration + 1) as f32).sqrt();
        for i in 0..k {
            if batch_counts[i] > 0 {
                let new_x = batch_sums[i][0] / batch_counts[i] as f32;
                let new_y = batch_sums[i][1] / batch_counts[i] as f32;
                let new_z = batch_sums[i][2] / batch_counts[i] as f32;

                centroids[i][0] = (1.0 - alpha) * centroids[i][0] + alpha * new_x;
                centroids[i][1] = (1.0 - alpha) * centroids[i][1] + alpha * new_y;
                centroids[i][2] = (1.0 - alpha) * centroids[i][2] + alpha * new_z;
            }
        }

        if !any_changed {
            break;
        }
    }

    assignments
}

fn nearest_centroid(point: &[f32; 3], centroids: &[[f32; 3]]) -> usize {
    let mut best_idx = 0;
    let mut best_dist = f32::INFINITY;

    for (i, centroid) in centroids.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = distance(point, centroid);
        if d < best_dist {
            best_dist = d;
            best_idx = i;
        }
    }

    best_idx
}

#[inline]
fn distance(p1: &[f32; 3], p2: &[f32; 3]) -> f32 {
    let dx = p1[0] - p2[0];
    let dy = p1[1] - p2[1];
    let dz = p1[2] - p2[2];
    dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
}
